title: Struggling Travel Stock for Options Bears to Bears to Snag
text: After a roughly 20% rally off their July low, the shares of Expedia Group Inc (NASDAQ:EXPE) are seeing failure at the 80-day moving average, as well as the site of the stock's May lows earlier this month.

title: Expedia (EXPE) Stock: Why It Fell 4.62%
text: The stock price of Expedia (EXPE) fell by 4.62% in the most recent trading session. This is why.

title: 3 Popular Stocks With Recent Price Target Cuts
text: Today's article introduces three stocks whose  price targets have recently been cut by analysts on Wall Street. So far this year, the stocks market has tumbled on the back of rampant inflation and investors' shaken confidence.

title: Can Expedia Stock Rebound After Almost A 40% Decline This Year?
text: After a 38% decline year-to-date, at the current price of around $114 per share, we believe Expedia's stock (NASDAQ: EXPE), a travel company providing everything from airline tickets, to hotel rooms, and car rentals - could see a modest rebound.

title: 7 Stocks to Buy to Easily Outperform the Market
text: The many experts who were extremely bearish on stocks in May and June predicted the second-quarter earnings season would be a total disaster. In fact, the earnings season, boosted by the resilience of many middle-class and wealthy American consumers, has been pretty good.

title: 3 Cruise Stocks to Buy on the Dip
text: When U.S. investors think about cruise stocks to buy on the dip, they usually think of the three main companies: Carnival (NYSE: CCL ), Royal Caribbean Cruises (NYSE: RCL ), and Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE: NCLH ). However, there are several cruise-related businesses that also make potentially attractive stock picks at this point in the market.

title: Expedia Group: Strong Travel Demand Keeps Us Bullish
text: Expedia reported Q2 earnings which beat expectations. The company is benefiting from strong travel demand that has remained resilient despite macro headwinds.

title: Expedia: Q2 2019 Vs. Q2 2022 - Something Doesn't Add Up
text: We compared the two quarters and something does not add up. Even if we are not at the pre-covid-19 level in air travel, Expedia is delivering better results.

title: Which Is A Better Buy: Booking Holdings Or Expedia?
text: Both Booking Holdings and Expedia are top players in the online travel services segment. With a recovery in tourism, both the companies look poised to flourish.

title: Expedia's bounceback in hotel bookings looks good — until you compare it to how many people are flocking back to Airbnb
text: Expedia reported an 8% rise in hotel bookings from 2019. Airbnb said its gross bookings rose 73% from 3 years ago, as it swung to a huge profit.

title: Expedia (EXPE) Q2 Earnings & Revenues Beat Estimates, Rise Y/Y
text: Expedia's (EXPE) second-quarter 2022 results benefit from strengthening travel demand amid the pandemic-led disruptions.

text: Expedia Group, Inc. title: Expedia (EXPE) Surpasses Q2 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: Expedia (EXPE) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of 24.84% and 4.78%, respectively, for the quarter ended June 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Expedia rides 'strong' travel rebound to earnings beat
text: Expedia Group Inc. shares EXPE, -3.27% were headed 6% higher in after-hours trading Thursday after the online-travel company topped expectations with its latest results amid a strong rebound in travel. The company reported a net loss of $185 million, or $1.17 a share, compared with a loss of $301 million, or $2.02 a share, in the year-before quarter.

title: Will Expedia's Stock Move Higher Post Fiscal Q2 Results?
text: Expedia (NASDAQ: EXPE), a travel company providing everything from airline tickets, hotel rooms, car rentals, to cruises, is scheduled to announce its fiscal second-quarter results on Thursday, August 4.

title: Expedia (EXPE) to Report Q2 Earnings: What's in the Offing?
text: Expedia's (EXPE) Q2 results are likely to reflect gains from increasing gross bookings owing to growing travel demand. Yet, uncertainties related to the pandemic remain a concern.
